questions.q: In Hindu mythology, what is the deity Kubera principally the lord of?
questions.explanation: Kubera is revered as the lord of riches and the treasurer of the gods.

questions.q: Which class of nature-spirits and demigods does Kubera rule over?
questions.explanation: Kubera is the king of the yakshas, the demigods associated with hidden treasures of the earth.

questions.q: Which cardinal direction is the deity Kubera traditionally said to guard?
questions.explanation: Kubera is the dikpala, or guardian deity, of the northern direction.

questions.q: In which mountain range is Kubera's celestial city of Alaka said to lie?
questions.explanation: Kubera rules from his city Alaka set in the Himalayas, near Mount Kailasa.

questions.q: What is the name of Kubera's celestial city in the Himalayas?
questions.explanation: Kubera rules the wealth of the earth from his city Alaka in the Himalayas.

questions.q: Near which sacred mountain, the abode of Shiva, is Kubera's city said to lie?
questions.explanation: Kubera's city Alaka lies in the Himalayas near Mount Kailasa, the abode of Shiva.

questions.q: In traditional iconography, how is the wealth-god Kubera most often depicted in form?
options: As a fierce warrior on horseback
As a plump figure seated with a money-pot
As a serpent coiled on the waters
questions.explanation: Kubera is commonly shown plump and seated, holding a money-pot symbolizing his treasures.

questions.q: The flying chariot Pushpaka was originally owned by which deity before it passed to others?
questions.explanation: The Pushpaka Vimana was the original possession of Kubera, the god of wealth.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, the demon-king Ravana is described as a half-brother of which god of wealth?
questions.explanation: Ravana and Kubera were half-brothers, both sons of the sage Vishrava.

questions.q: Kubera is honored as the treasurer of whom?
questions.explanation: Kubera serves as the treasurer of the gods, guarding the wealth of the cosmos.

questions.q: Which sage is named in the tradition as the father of both Kubera and Ravana?
questions.explanation: The sage Vishrava was the father of Kubera and, by another wife, of Ravana.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, what becomes of Kubera's flying chariot Pushpaka after Ravana seizes it?
options: It is destroyed in the war at Lanka
It is given permanently to Hanuman
It sinks into the ocean of milk
It is reclaimed and returns to Kubera after Ravana's fall
questions.explanation: After Ravana's defeat the Pushpaka bore Rama back to Ayodhya and afterward returned to its owner Kubera.

questions.q: Which divine architect is said in the Ramayana to have fashioned the Pushpaka chariot for Kubera?
questions.explanation: The Ramayana credits the divine architect Vishvakarma with creating the Pushpaka Vimana for Kubera.

questions.q: By what relationship is the demon-king Ravana connected to Kubera in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Ravana is described as the half-brother of Kubera, both being sons of the sage Vishrava.

questions.q: As the guardian of the northern quarter, Kubera belongs to which group of directional deities?
questions.explanation: Kubera is counted among the Lokapalas or dikpalas, the guardians of the directions, holding the north.

questions.q: The wealth that Kubera guards is principally associated with which realm?
options: The wealth hidden in the earth
questions.explanation: Kubera guards the wealth of the earth, including its buried treasures and minerals.

questions.q: Which object is most characteristically held by Kubera in his depictions as the god of wealth?
questions.explanation: Kubera is typically shown holding a money-pot, an emblem of the riches he commands.

questions.q: Over which group of demigods does Kubera reign as king from his city in the north?
questions.explanation: Kubera reigns as king of the yakshas, demigods linked to hidden wealth.

questions.q: Which deity is regarded as the divine treasurer who safeguards the riches of the gods?
questions.explanation: Kubera holds the office of treasurer of the gods, guarding their wealth.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, after Ravana's defeat the Pushpaka chariot first carried Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana to which city?
questions.explanation: The reclaimed Pushpaka bore Rama, Sita, and Lakshmana back to Ayodhya before returning to Kubera.

questions.q: Kubera's role as keeper of treasures most closely parallels the function of which goddess in Hindu tradition?
options: Lakshmi, goddess of wealth and fortune
questions.explanation: Both Kubera and Lakshmi are associated with wealth and prosperity in Hindu tradition.

questions.q: Which famous self-moving aerial chariot is associated in mythology with Kubera as its original owner?
questions.explanation: The Pushpaka Vimana, a self-moving aerial chariot, was originally the possession of Kubera.

questions.q: By what patronymic, referring to his father Vishrava, is Kubera sometimes known?
questions.explanation: Kubera is called Vaishravana, a patronymic meaning son of Vishrava.

questions.q: In the Ramayana's account, by what means did Ravana come to possess the Pushpaka chariot that had belonged to Kubera?
options: He won it in a contest of austerities
It was gifted to him by Brahma
He purchased it from the yakshas
He seized it by force from his half-brother Kubera
questions.explanation: Ravana forcibly took the Pushpaka chariot from his half-brother Kubera and kept it at Lanka.

questions.q: Which mount or vehicle is most commonly attributed to Kubera in iconographic tradition?
questions.explanation: Kubera is traditionally described with the nara vahana, a man as his mount, fitting his lordship over yakshas.

questions.q: Kubera's ruling seat in Alaka is set apart from the courts of which other directional kings, such as the eastern king of the devas?
questions.explanation: Indra rules the east as king of the devas, while Kubera holds the northern quarter from Alaka.

questions.q: In Kubera's office as guardian of the north, which neighboring quarter and its guardian lies opposite to him?
questions.explanation: Kubera holds the north while Yama, lord of death, guards the opposite southern quarter.

questions.q: Which jewel-laden epithet, reflecting his lordship over treasures, is applied to Kubera as the giver of wealth?
questions.explanation: Kubera bears the epithet Dhanapati, lord of wealth, reflecting his command over riches.

questions.q: In the tradition, who held the Pushpaka chariot before Ravana, and to whom did it finally return after the war in the Ramayana?
options: It belonged to and returned to Kubera
It belonged to and returned to Indra
It belonged to and returned to Brahma
It belonged to and returned to Vishvakarma
questions.explanation: The Pushpaka originally belonged to Kubera, was seized by Ravana, and returned to Kubera after the war.

questions.q: Kubera's lordship over hidden riches makes him a patron of which broad domain in Hindu thought?
questions.explanation: As lord of riches, Kubera is the patron of prosperity and material abundance.
